assumptions: The intragroup dividend has already been declared and recognised as an intragroup receivable/payable, so the fact pattern involves a recognised intragroup monetary item rather than a forecast transaction.

The receivable/payable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange gains or losses that are not fully eliminated on consolidation because the group entities involved have different functional currencies.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: It is the primary hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability exposed to foreign exchange risk that could affect profit or loss at the consolidated level.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: It is the other IFRS 9 hedge accounting model that can apply to a recognised asset or liability when the exposure is framed as variability in settlement cash flows from foreign exchange risk.
